title: `deque_clear` clears `MemoryError`
author: brijkapadia
body:
# Bug report

### Bug description:


This is a sub-issue for gh-146102 with the gist [here](https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/c0e661ad36a12581333740414e20098e).
---

In the case that there is no memory, `deque_clear` calls `PyErr_Clear()`:
https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/7928a8b730b0334c9f97c2144ab00fe0d88f1e97/Modules/_collectionsmodule.c#L749-L753

This was done to clear the error that  `newblock` raises. However, if there was originally an exception, `deque_clear` clears it.

### CPython versions tested on:

CPython main branch

### Operating systems tested on:

Linux

<!-- gh-linked-prs -->
### Linked PRs
* gh-152018
<!-- /gh-linked-prs -->

comments.author: rhettinger
comments.body:
What is wrong with the current code?  If an allocation fails due to lack of memory (a very rare almost non-existent event), then an alternate path is taken that does not require additional memory.

AFAICT this is a false positive from the umbrella issue.

comments.author: brijkapadia
comments.body:
> AFAICT this is a false positive from the umbrella issue. There is a real error here that the reproducer catches. However, the umbrella issue gives the wrong explanation. The problem has nothing to do with `deque.copy` but `deque.copy` is one execution path for the error.
Reproducer (for reference)

```python import _testcapi from collections import deque for n in range(1, 80): d = deque(range(200)) _testcapi.set_nomemory(n, 0) try: d2 = d.copy() _testcapi.remove_mem_hooks() break except MemoryError: _testcapi.remove_mem_hooks() except: _testcapi.remove_mem_hooks() # Fatal: _Py_Dealloc: Deallocator of type 'collections.deque' cleared ```

The real problem is that this debug check in `_Py_Dealloc` fails:
Debug Check

https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/1812162f81ef034616294ec9ee53e951f99c9582/Objects/object.c#L3321-L3342

--- For the specific case of the reproducer, `deque_copy_impl` calls `deque_append_lock_held` and in the case of an error, calls `Py_DECREF(new_deque)`: https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/1812162f81ef034616294ec9ee53e951f99c9582/Modules/_collectionsmodule.c#L634-L638 Furthermore, `deque_append_lock_held` calls `newblock` which raises a `PyErr_NoMemory()` in the case of OOM: https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/1812162f81ef034616294ec9ee53e951f99c9582/Modules/_collectionsmodule.c#L187 Thus, in the case of OOM, `Py_DECREF(new_deque)` is called with an exception already set. In order to free the memory, eventually `deque_clear` is called. `deque_clear` calls `newblock`, which in the case of OOM, replaces the current exception with a new `PyErr_NoMemory()`. Then, `deque_clear` calls `PyErr_Clear()`, meaning that there are now no exceptions despite `deque_append_lock_held` originally setting one: https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/1812162f81ef034616294ec9ee53e951f99c9582/Modules/_collectionsmodule.c#L749-L753 In debug builds, this is caught in `_Py_Dealloc`, https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/1812162f81ef034616294ec9ee53e951f99c9582/Objects/object.c#L3329-L3331 which gives the error `Fatal: _Py_Dealloc: Deallocator of type 'collections.deque' cleared the current exception`.

comments.author: stestagg
comments.body:
EDIT: ignore my previous, it was something else.

I just came across this in fuzzing, the following code reliably aborts for me:

```python
import collections
d =33333333333* collections.deque([10])
```

gives (in linux with 2gb ram)

```
Quitting LLDB will kill one or more processes. Do you really want to proceed: [Y/n] y
~ # /pfm/tools/mem_limit_exec /pfm/py/bin/python3 /pfm/scratch/reproducers/t1-1.
min.py
Fatal Python error: _Py_Dealloc: Deallocator of type 'collections.deque' cleared the current exception
Python runtime state: initialized

Current thread 0x0000fffff7fee020 [python3] (most recent call first):
  File "<input-53>", line 2 in <module>
  File "/pfm/scratch/reproducers/t1-1.min.py", line 10 in <module>
Aborted
~ # /pfm/tools/mem_limit_exec /pfm/py/bin/python3 /pfm/scratch/reproducers/t1-1.
min.py
Fatal Python error: _Py_Dealloc: Deallocator of type 'collections.deque' cleared the current exception
Python runtime state: initialized

Current thread 0x0000fffff7fee020 [python3] (most recent call first):

Aborted
```
